div1.entryFree: Stride, subst. a step; especially a long and proud step: turn two mincing steps into a manly s. Merch. III, 4, 68. every tedious s. R2 I, 3, 268. every s. he makes upon my land is dangerous treason, III, 3, 92. a s. and a stand, Troil. III, 3, 252. follow his --s, Tim. I, 1, 80. the --s they victors made, Cymb. V, 3, 43. In Mcb. II, 1, 55 M. Edd. --s, O. Edd. sides.
